rationale_for_baseline_tier: A painless, non-necrotic, non-ulcerated porcelain-white atrophic plaque with only self-induced blanching erythema in a well patient is benign extragenital lichen sclerosus — low acuity, managed with reassurance, stopping the rubbing, gentle emollient and routine GP/dermatology confirmation. The only escalation is for necrosis/infection, which is absent.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Area breaks into a bleeding ulcer, turns black, becomes hot/painful with spreading redness, or fever develops
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same-day GP/urgent care (ED if systemically unwell)
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate necrosis or secondary infection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the patch breaks into a bleeding sore, turns black, or becomes hot and painful with spreading redness or a fever, get seen the same day (ED if you feel unwell).

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Partner can help her disengage from repeated inspection.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is a benign, slow skin condition (lichen sclerosus) — not necrosis or a spreading infection. Stop rubbing it (the red ring will fade), use a gentle moisturiser, and see your GP in 2-3 weeks to confirm and get a treatment cream. Get seen the same day if it breaks into a bleeding ulcer, turns black, or becomes hot and painful with a fever.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within 2-3 weeks; same-day if necrosis/infection signs
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: No emergency care needed unless a necrosis/infection red flag appears.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Telehealth impression of benign extragenital lichen sclerosus with self-induced reactive erythema; necrosis/vasculitis/infection excluded; reassurance and gentle emollient advised; routine dermatological confirmation (vs morphea) and topical steroid consideration recommended.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring without a necrosis/infection screen or safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A genuinely necrotic or infected lesion is missed because red flags were never screened.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: ED escalation for a painless benign white plaque is over-triage and reinforces panic.
